document_summary.executive_summary: PaymentHub API is an internal REST API that provides a unified payment initiation and reconciliation layer for the bank's lending platform, consolidating direct debit, card payment, and bank transfer logic into a single service consumed by all product teams. It addresses duplicated validation logic across three independent microservices, inconsistent error handling causing 2.3% payment failure misclassification, and the absence of a unified payment status view across 45,000 daily transactions. The expected outcome is a 60% reduction in payment integration effort for new products, elimination of duplicate payment incidents, and a single PSD2-compliant SCA enforcement point with PCI-DSS audit trail.

content.execution_summary:
• Generated vision document for PaymentHub API
• 5 sections: Executive Summary, Business Context, Full Scope Vision, MVP Scope, Risks & Dependencies
• 5 feature areas: Payment Initiation, Reconciliation & Settlement, SCA Orchestration, Payment Status & Events, Audit & Compliance
• 9 MVP features (Must Have: 7, Should Have: 2)
• 7 risks identified (migration without downtime: critical, PSD2 SCA timing: high, GoCardless rate limits: medium)
• 6 open questions flagged for requirements analysis
• Grounded in kb-L2-payments-domain (88% coverage — PSD2 rules, payment types, settlement cycles, reconciliation patterns from KB)
• Reflection: fixed 1 feature that prescribed a message queue (removed tech decision), added FCA CAB constraint to MVP Constraints, clarified that no new database is owned
• Uploaded to /f7e8d9c0-b1a2-3456-7890-abcdef123456/vision-doc/vision-paymenthub-api.md
